explanation: The property states that globally (G), if a parameter value is less than its minimum defined alarm threshold (param_V_lessThan_paramMin_V) OR if a parameter value is greater than its maximum defined alarm threshold (param_V_greaterThan_paramMax_V), then an alarm for that erroneous parameter value (paramAlarm_V) must be active. This directly captures the requirement that an alarm shall be raised when a parameter value goes outside its defined range.

The property states that globally (G), if a parameter value goes outside its defined range (either less than the minimum or greater than the maximum), then a parameter alarm must be active. This directly translates 'The system shall raise an alarm when a parameter value goes outside the range defined for its associated alarm' into an LTL implication.

The property states that globally, if a parameter value goes outside its defined range (either less than the minimum or greater than the maximum), then an alarm indicating an erroneous parameter value must be active. This ensures that the alarm is raised concurrently with the parameter being out of range.

The property states that globally (G), if a parameter value goes outside its defined range (either less than the minimum or greater than the maximum), then a parameter alarm must be active. This directly captures the requirement that an alarm is raised when the parameter value is out of range.

The property states that globally, if a parameter value is either less than its minimum defined alarm threshold or greater than its maximum defined alarm threshold (bool_exp1), then an alarm for an erroneous parameter value must be active (bool_exp3). This directly captures the requirement that an alarm shall be raised when a parameter value goes outside its defined range.

The property states that globally, if a parameter value is less than its minimum defined threshold or greater than its maximum defined threshold (bool_exp1), then an alarm for an erroneous parameter value must be active (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that an alarm is raised when a parameter goes outside its defined range.

The property states that globally, if a parameter's value is outside its defined range (meaning it is either less than its minimum or greater than its maximum), then an alarm indicating an erroneous parameter value must be active. This captures the 'when X, then Y' causality of the requirement, implying an immediate or concurrent activation of the alarm.

The property states that globally, it must always be the case that if a parameter value is less than its minimum defined value OR a parameter value is greater than its maximum defined value (representing the condition of being outside the defined range), then an alarm for an erroneous parameter value must be active at the same time. This ensures that an alarm is raised immediately when a parameter goes out of its specified range.

The property states that globally, if a parameter value goes below its minimum or above its maximum, then an alarm for an erroneous parameter value must be raised. This directly translates the requirement that the system shall raise an alarm when a parameter value goes outside the defined range.

The property states that globally, if a parameter's value goes outside its defined range (either less than the minimum or greater than the maximum), then an alarm for an erroneous parameter value must be raised.
